name: Confirmation (reference number)
pageTitle: Import notification sent - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
bodyCopy: You must use the correct reference and code on the customs declaration or your consignment will be delayed.
Tell the haulier that this consignment must go directly to London Tilbury when it enters Great Britain.
If the consignment does not arrive in Great Britain at the entry point stated in this notification, it must go directly to the Border Control Post where it first arrives in Great Britain.
An inspector may update the risk assessment.
If the risk assessment changes, IPAFFS will notify you and any nominated contacts by text message or email.
How to view or amend this notification
You can view or amend this notification from your dashboard.
Tell us what we can improve
secondaryActions: Copy (x3 — one per reference row: CHED reference, Reference for your customs declaration, Customs document code)
What did you think of this service? (opens in new tab) — external Qualtrics feedback link

structure.govukComponent: Notification banner (custom modifier notification-banner--inspection-required; header only, no __content)

structure.text: Inspection status / Required at London Tilbury
structure.govukComponent: Panel (custom modifier panel--inspection-required; inline border-top:none, replaces the standard confirmation title/body)

structure.text: CHED reference :: CHEDP.GB.2026.1525979 :: Copy | Reference for your customs declaration :: GBCHD2026.1525979 :: Copy | Customs document code :: N853 :: Copy
structure.govukComponent: Summary list (id=review-table-about-notification, 3 rows, each row has a Copy button in the actions column)

structure.text: You must use the correct reference and code on the customs declaration or your consignment will be delayed.
Tell the haulier that this consignment must go directly to London Tilbury when it enters Great Britain.
If the consignment does not arrive in Great Britain at the entry point stated in this notification, it must go directly to the Border Control Post where it first arrives in Great Britain.
An inspector may update the risk assessment.
If the risk assessment changes, IPAFFS will notify you and any nominated contacts by text message or email.
How to view or amend this notification
You can view or amend this notification from your dashboard.
Tell us what we can improve
What did you think of this service? (opens in new tab)

govukComponents.usage: Header-only banner titled 'Initial risk assessment' announcing the inspection outcome. Only the __header/h2 is present — no standard govuk-notification-banner__content region.

Confirmation panel showing 'Inspection status' / 'Required at London Tilbury'. Uses inline style border-top:none and a custom modifier — it does NOT use the standard green govuk-panel--confirmation with a govuk-panel__title. There is no H1/panel title on this variant.

Displays the three reference codes as key/value rows with a Copy button in the actions column of each row: CHED reference (id=reference-number), Reference for your customs declaration (id=reference-number-customs), Customs document code (id=reference-number-document).
Emphasises that the correct reference/code must be used on the customs declaration.

Three 'Copy' buttons (also carry custom class copy-button + data-copy-value) that copy each reference code to the clipboard. type=submit, data-prevent-double-click. Confirmed attribute set per button: data-module, data-prevent-double-click, type, id, class, data-copy-value, data-govuk-button-init (no data-copy). NOTE (a11y defect): the first two copy buttons SHARE the same id 'copy-customs-declaration-reference-number' (duplicate id in the DOM); only the third differs (copy-customs-doc-code). A rebuild must give each button a unique id.
Visible horizontal rule separating the reference block from the 'What you need to do' guidance.
External feedback link to Qualtrics ('What did you think of this service? (opens in new tab)').

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom modifier + title class on the GOV.UK notification banner, used to render the 'Initial risk assessment' header and colour-code the banner by inspection outcome.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke IPAFFS styling layered on the standard component; the confirmation outcome is expressed via a custom banner+panel combination rather than a standard confirmation panel/H1. The new app will need an equivalent way to signal inspection-required vs not-required outcomes.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-notification-banner (default or --success variant) with a standard govuk-panel--confirmation title; the risk/inspection state can be conveyed through banner variant + heading copy without custom CSS.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom-styled govuk-panel showing 'Inspection status / Required at London Tilbury'; strips the top border and overrides the standard green confirmation panel look.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Non-standard panel styling + an inline style attribute. This variant has NO H1/panel title at all, which is unusual for a GOV.UK confirmation page (the accessible page name relies solely on the <title>).
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Standard govuk-panel--confirmation with a govuk-panel__title (H1) e.g. 'Notification sent', plus body text for the inspection status.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: copy-button class + data-copy-value attribute on govuk-button
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Client-side clipboard-copy widget: each reference row has a 'Copy' button carrying data-copy-value; JS copies that value to the clipboard. (The only copy-related attribute in the DOM is data-copy-value — there is no data-copy attribute.)
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Requires bespoke client JS and degrades without it (button is type=submit). Not part of the GOV.UK Design System — a rebuild would need to decide whether copy-to-clipboard is worth a custom progressive-enhancement component.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: No native GOV.UK component; the reference is already selectable text in the summary list, so the Copy buttons are an optional enhancement rather than a requirement.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: link-button (id=manage-notifications, and the 'Create a new notification' link)
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Anchor styled to look like/behave as an action link for the two exit navigation options.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Custom class rather than a standard govuk-link or govuk-button; mixes link and button semantics.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-link for navigation, or govuk-button (data-module=govuk-button) if it should read as a primary action.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom wrapper block holding the 'Tell us what we can improve' heading and external feedback link.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke IPAFFS layout class; the underlying content is standard govuk-heading-s + govuk-link so it is easily reproducible.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Plain govuk-grid-row / govuk-body markup — no custom class needed.

fields.name: reference-number
fields.label: CHED reference
fields.evidence: db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 175 (assert #reference-number matches ^CHEDP\.GB\.\d{4}\.\d{7,8}$; rendered read-only value in a summary-list __value, NOT an input). CORROBORATED: page-objects/notification/ConfirmationPage.ts:6-8,30-38 (locatorReferenceNumber = '#reference-number', referenceNumber() reads its textContent); every ched-p spec asserts toContainText(chedReferencePattern(chedType.chedP)) e.g. tests/notification/ched-p/ched-p-notification.spec.ts:38; the pattern is defined in types/ched-type.ts:17 as ^CHEDP\.GB\.\d{4}\.\d{7,8}$ — identical to the trace regex. This is the ONLY reference control the page object / tests know about; #reference-number-customs and #reference-number-document below are trace-only (not exercised by any test).

fields.name: reference-number-customs
fields.label: Reference for your customs declaration
fields.evidence: db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 175 (read-only summary-list value; format GBCHD{year}.{serial})

fields.name: reference-number-document
fields.label: Customs document code
fields.evidence: db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 175 (read-only summary-list value; N853 is the customs document code for CHED-P/fish). NOT corroborated by any test/page object — trace-only.

fields.name: risk-assessment-outcome
fields.label: Inspection status
fields.evidence: TRACE db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 175 rendered the 'Inspection required' variant ('Inspection status' / 'Required at London Tilbury') via panel--inspection-required. This field EXISTS and displays the post-submission risk-assessment outcome — confirmed. The FOUR possible outcome values are INFERRED (only one traced): page-objects/notification/ConfirmationPage.ts:14-54 exposes getRiskAssessmentStatus() distinguishing 'Check GVMS' (getByText 'Check GVMS'), 'Inspection required' (getByText /Required at /), 'Inspection not required' (getByText 'Not required'), 'Go to place of destination' (getByText 'Go to place of destination'). tests/notification/ched-p/ched-p-inspection-non-transit.spec.ts:19-101 parametrises all four across risk-category / GVMS / CTC combinations and asserts each on this page. The other three variants and their exact rendered copy (banner/panel titles, guidance body) are a gap — not in our trace corpus.

openQuestions: This confirmation page is read-only (post-submission) — it has NO form controls, back link, or primary submit button, and (unusually for GOV.UK) NO H1/panel title. The three 'fields' captured are read-only OUTPUT reference codes shown in a summary list, not inputs. fieldCount reflects those outputs.

The captured trace is a CHED-P fish notification and the evidence is the exact IUU carrier (IPAFFS files IUU/fishery products as CHED-P). References use the CHEDP prefix and customs document code N853. The new standalone IUU app will need its own reference-number scheme/prefix and to confirm the correct customs document code(s) for IUU catch-certificate consignments.

This is the INSPECTION-REQUIRED variant (panel--inspection-required / notification-banner--inspection-required, 'Inspection status: Required at London Tilbury'). The QA repo confirms there are at least FOUR distinct post-submission risk-assessment outcomes rendered on this page — 'Check GVMS', 'Inspection required' (traced), 'Inspection not required' ('Not required'), and 'Go to place of destination' (page-objects/notification/ConfirmationPage.ts:14-54; tests/notification/ched-p/ched-p-inspection-non-transit.spec.ts:19-101 asserts all four). Only 'Inspection required' was in our trace corpus; the exact banner/panel/guidance copy for the other three outcomes is a GAP a human must supply. The new IUU app must decide how to signal each outcome (the traced variant uses a bespoke custom-modifier banner+panel rather than the standard green confirmation panel).

Corroboration of the outcome inputs (from the workflow, for context on how the outcome is driven — NOT a requirement of this page): risk category + entry BCP + GVMS-transport + CTC selection determine the resulting risk assessment (ched-p-workflows.ts:645-651; ChedPNonTransitConfig at :586-601). This is legacy IPAFFS risk-engine behaviour; the confirmation page only DISPLAYS the outcome.

ACCESSIBILITY REQUIREMENT (inferred): the confirmation page must pass an axe accessibility scan — tests/accessibility/ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:283-284 runs checkPageAccessibility(page, 'Confirmation Page'). Note this is in tension with the nonStandardPatterns finding that the traced variant has NO H1/panel title; the a11y test passed, so the accessible page name is carried by the document <title> alone. A rebuild should give the confirmation a proper H1.

The 'Return to your dashboard' exit link IS corroborated as a real, exercised control (page-objects/notification/ConfirmationPage.ts:10-12 linkReturnToDashboard by role 'link'; clicked in ched-p-workflows.ts:1351 after reading the reference). The Copy buttons, 'Create a new notification' link and Qualtrics feedback link are trace-only (no test drives them) — treat as legacy chrome to confirm.

Reference formats observed: CHED reference = CHEDP.GB.2026.1525979 (regex ^CHEDP\.GB\.\d{4}\.\d{7,8}$), customs declaration reference = GBCHD2026.1525979, customs document code = N853. Whether the second/third are derived from the first is not shown here.

The 'Return to your dashboard' and 'Create a new notification' exit links, the Copy buttons, and the Qualtrics feedback link are all IPAFFS-specific navigation/enhancement — a rebuild should confirm which of these are actual requirements vs legacy chrome.
